summary: Baby Drew Berry Clothing is a direct-to-consumer e-commerce boutique selling soft baby and toddler apparel — bodysuits, two-piece sets, dresses, pajamas, bamboo viscose garments, swimwear, and accessories for ages 0–3. It serves quality-conscious new and expecting parents (primarily moms 25–40) who care about fabric safety for sensitive skin, plus gift buyers and repeat parents. Discovery runs through Instagram and flash-sale urgency; purchases are one-off, same-day, self-serve checkout, with repeat buying driven organically by babies sizing up every 2–4 months.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Post-Purchase Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Reach first value — bridge the gap between checkout and first wear, reassure the parent they made a great choice, and prime the social-share moment.
campaigns.trigger: First purchase / order placed (send email 1 within seconds of checkout).
campaigns.segment: All new buyers; branch later by first-time buyer vs. gift buyer vs. repeat parent.
campaigns.rationale: Nothing exists today and the 3–7 day pre-delivery window is this brand's highest-anxiety, highest-engagement period — exactly when buyer's remorse forms and the 'can't feel the fabric' doubt peaks. A warm welcome that reassures the quality choice converts a one-off impulse buy into a relationship.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome + reassure the quality decision + set delivery expectations + plant the One Love story
emails.subject: Your little one's softest layer is on its way 💛
emails.preview: A note from our family to yours — and what to expect before it lands.
keyPoints: Thank them warmly, parent-to-parent, in the 'One Love' family voice — not a corporate receipt
Reassure the choice: bamboo-soft, made for sensitive skin, the softness they couldn't feel online is real
Set expectations: arriving in 3–7 days, with a gentle teaser that fabric-care tips are coming so it stays cloud-soft
One link only — track the order — keeping the moment calm and single-focus

campaigns.name: Fabric Guide Lead-Magnet Nurture
campaigns.objective: Build the email list that doesn't exist yet and move a pre-purchase subscriber toward their first order over a short cycle.
campaigns.trigger: Email opt-in via a gated lead magnet (e.g., 'Bamboo vs. Cotton: A Parent's Fabric Safety Guide' or a baby sizing guide).
campaigns.segment: New subscribers who have not yet purchased; branch by topic of the magnet downloaded.
campaigns.rationale: There is no email lead magnet or opt-in today — Instagram is the only soft capture, which the brand doesn't own. A fabric-education magnet directly defuses the #1 objection ('I can't feel the fabric before I buy'), turning the brand's core differentiator into a list-building and trust engine.

emails.purpose: Deliver the asset instantly + frame the sensitive-skin problem it solves + introduce the brand's answer
emails.subject: Bamboo or cotton? Here's what actually touches your baby's skin
emails.preview: Your free fabric guide is inside — plus the one label detail most parents miss.
keyPoints: Deliver the promised guide above the fold — no friction, no upsell first
Frame the real problem: harsh synthetics irritate newborn skin and fast-fashion fabrics pill after a few washes
Address the top objection head-on — how to judge softness and safety when you can't touch it through a screen
Soft, single next step to browse the bamboo line; no discount, lead with value

campaigns.name: Size-Up & Milestone Repurchase
campaigns.objective: Capture the natural repurchase cycle by reminding parents when their baby is ready for the next size, and expand basket value with the premium bamboo line and accessories.
campaigns.trigger: Time since first purchase / baby-age milestone (3m, 6m, 9m, 12m, 18m, 24m) — fires roughly every 2–4 months.
campaigns.segment: Past buyers approaching a size-up window; branch by purchased size and by buyer type.
campaigns.rationale: Babies outgrow a size every 2–4 months — the single biggest repeat-revenue lever this brand has — yet there is no system to catch it. The real churn risk is brand forgetting in that gap; a well-timed size-up nudge turns organic growth into reliable repeat orders.

emails.purpose: Re-engage at the size-up moment, make the next size effortless to shop, and gently upsell the premium bamboo line
emails.subject: Growing fast? It's about time to size up the softness
emails.preview: Most babies outgrow a size every 2–3 months — here's what fits now.
keyPoints: Lead with the milestone — celebrate how fast their little one is growing, not a hard sell
Make the next size one tap away; remove the friction that causes parents to drift to another brand
Upsell gently to the premium bamboo viscose line as the natural 'next stage' soft layer
Low-friction add-ons — hats, headbands, socks, mittens — framed as easy completers

campaigns.name: Monthly Value Newsletter
campaigns.objective: Stay top-of-mind across the long gaps between purchases and build authority without leaning on discounts.
campaigns.segment: All engaged contacts; suppress active onboarding buyers and route recently-dormant contacts to win-back instead.
campaigns.rationale: The brand already produces SEO blog content (authored by Jill) that is never emailed — a ready-made authority asset going unused. A monthly note keeps the brand present during the 2–4 month purchase gap, the exact window where parents forget and shop elsewhere.

emails.purpose: Deliver one genuinely useful parenting/fabric idea (repurposed from the blog) with a single soft CTA
emails.subject: The 3-second softness test every parent should know
emails.preview: This month: a quick fabric check, a new drop, and a little love from our family.
keyPoints: One useful idea per send — a practical fabric-care or sensitive-skin tip, repurposed from existing blog content
80% value / 20% soft offer — earn the open with help, not a sale
A recurring habit-building section (e.g., 'This month's little-one tip') readers come to expect
Single primary CTA to read the full piece; weave in the One Love story to keep it warm and human

campaigns.name: Lapsed-Parent Win-Back
campaigns.objective: Reactivate parents who haven't purchased or engaged in months and clean the list to protect deliverability.
campaigns.trigger: Inactivity threshold — 90+ days with no purchase, opens, or clicks.
campaigns.segment: Dormant past buyers; branch separately from never-purchased dormant subscribers.
campaigns.rationale: With repeat buying purely organic and no re-engagement mechanism, lapsed parents quietly drift away — often just because the baby grew and the brand was forgotten. A gentle 'we've missed your little one' nudge with what's new recovers a meaningful slice (5–15%) before they're gone for good.

emails.purpose: Warm 'we miss you' reconnection that reminds them of the core value and surfaces what's new since they left
emails.subject: We've missed your little one 💛
emails.preview: It's been a while — and your baby has grown. See what's new since your last visit.
keyPoints: Lead with warmth, no guilt — acknowledge that life with a growing baby gets busy
Remind them of the core value: the cloud-soft, sensitive-skin-safe fabric they came for
Surface fresh collections and new sizes — tie it to the likelihood their baby has grown into a new range
One gentle CTA back to the shop; reserve the sunset 'should we stop emailing you?' ask for a later email in the flow
